title: keysseria radicans subsp. radicans
intro: Keysseria radicans subsp. radicans, commonly known as Poison Ivy, is a plant species native to North America, characterized by its shiny green leaves with a distinctive white or yellowish color on the underside. The plant contains urushiol, a resinous oil with medicinal properties that have been used to treat various health conditions. The medicinal properties of Poison Ivy include anti-inflammatory, antiseptic, and antiviral properties, which can help alleviate skin conditions such as eczema and acne, as well as reduce the severity of colds and flu. Herbal preparations of Poison Ivy include teas, salves, and tinctures, which can be used topically or taken internally under the guidance of a medical professional.
